response.justification: The candidate is a strong, broadly experienced senior engineer with deep open-source involvement and solid Linux/architecture exposure, but is a poor fit for this executive leadership role. German is listed as basic while the role requires C1 minimum — a hard disqualifier for a client-facing consulting lead position. There is also no formal experience leading or managing teams (only individual-contributor roles plus a side-project founder role), and no evidence of Python, UML, or formal project management practice.
strengths: Extensive, varied software development experience across web, mobile, embedded, and high-performance systems
Genuine open-source commitment (founder of GISCollective, multiple maintained libraries, conference talks)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing culture, Linux environments
concerns: German only at basic level vs. required C1 minimum
No formal team leadership, mentoring-at-scale, or technical project management track record
No demonstrated Python, UML, or requirements-management experience listed

key_factors.factor: Deep open-source and Linux engineering background
